target.name: Xsight Labs
target.founder: Guy Koren, Erez Shaizaf, Gal Malach

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series D → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.5x) to $281M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $529M - $5.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 20.3x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: SambaNova Systems
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series D), post-money $5B

private.name: Tenstorrent
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series D), post-money $2.7B

data.narrative:
Xsight Labs is a Series D semiconductor company that has raised $281M to date, valued within a comp universe of 3 public and 8 private frontier-chip firms. The applicable peer set comprises 4 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage companies: SambaNova Systems ($5.0B), Tenstorrent ($2.7B), d-Matrix ($2.0B), and DEEPX ($529M).

Two valuation methods triangulate a range. Stage-progression applies a 1.5x step-up multiplier to the $281M total raised proxy, implying $422M. Peer-median anchors to the four-company cohort's median valuation of $2.4B, with a range spanning $529M to $5.0B. Public-comp anchoring via semiconductor P/S multiples (20.3x median across ALAB, NVDA, AMD) is inapplicable due to absence of target revenue disclosure. Blending stage-progression and peer-median methods yields a triangulated low of $358M, median of $1.4B, and high of $2.7B.

The wide range reflects material uncertainty around Xsight's competitive positioning within a heterogeneous peer cohort. The $2.7B high estimate aligns with mid-range peers like Tenstorrent; the $358M low reflects stage-based progression alone. A Series D funding round and customer traction would be critical data points to narrow this range and assess proximity to the peer median of $2.4B.
